intro: Ammi visnaga, also known as Khella, is a plant-based herbal remedy extracted from the seeds of the Ammi visnaga plant. Traditionally used in ancient Egypt and Greece, ammi visnaga tablets have been employed to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to their expectorant properties. However, modern medical research and usage of ammi visnaga are limited, and its effectiveness and potential side effects should be carefully evaluated before using it as a treatment.
side_effects: Ammi visnaga tablet can have side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and potential interactions with other medications. These side effects can vary in severity and may be more common in individuals with pre-existing medical conditions. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using ammi visnaga tablets to understand the potential risks and benefits.
uses: Ammi visnaga tablet is used to treat digestive issues, particularly related to excessive gas and bloating. It is also used to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Additionally, ammi visnaga tablet may be used to reduce inflammation and ease menstrual cramps.
heavy_periods: Ammi visnaga tablet is sometimes used to help alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding due to its alleged uterine stimulant properties. It may help to regulate menstrual flow and reduce blood loss. However, its effectiveness and safety for this purpose are not extensively scientifically proven.
fever: Ammi visnaga tablet is a herbal remedy traditionally used to treat fever and other symptoms of cold and flu. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms of fever. However, its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied, and its use should be approached with caution.
recipe: To make ammi visnaga tablet for medicinal use, first, dry the leaves and flowers of Ammi visnaga under shade or using a drying oven at 40-50 degrees Celsius for 2-3 days to preserve their medicinal properties. Next, grind the dried material into a fine powder. Then, mix the powder with an excipient like gum arabic or starch to form a tablet.

plant: Ammi visnaga tablet plant, also known as Khella or Bishop's weed, is an annual or biennial plant native to North Africa and the Mediterranean region.
